name: Fluciclovine (18F)
description: Fluciclovine is a [18F]-tagged synthetic analog of the amino acid L-leucine. It presents excellent diagnostic properties to be used in positron emission tomography (PET) imaging.[A31384] The structure of fluciclovine allows it to be uptaken by the tumoral cells by its amino acid transporter without incorporating in the metabolism within the body.[A31385] Fluciclovine was developed by Blue Earth Diagnostics, Ltd. and FDA approved in May 27, 2016.[L1049]
indication: Fluciclovine is indicated as a detection agent for positron emission tomography (PET) in men with suspected prostate cancer recurrence based on elevated blood prostate specific antigen (PSA) levels following prior treatment.[A31388] The overexpression of L-type amino acid transporters such as LAT1 and LAT3 that mediate the uptake of essential amino acids has been extensively reported as a tumoral mechanism of cell growth.[A31389]
toxicity: The hasn't been long-term carcinogenity or fertility studies in animals. Even though all reports have shown no mutagenicity, fluciclovine has the potential to be mutagenic.[L1052]
mechanismOfAction: Fluciclovine is transported into the prostate cancer cells via ASCT2 and LAT1 transporters. The activity of LAT1 gets increased in acidic pH, condition that is developed intra-tumorally at certain size. The uptake of fluciclovine presents an androgen-dependent dynamic in hormone sensitive cells.[L1051]
pharmacodynamics: Following intravenous administration, the tumor-to-normal tissue contrast is highest between 2 and 10 minutes after injection, with a 63% reduction in mean tumor uptake at 90 minutes after injection.[A31390] The scanning time point should be evaluated carefully as an early scanning can present an increased blood pool and a late scanning will translate into an increased muscle uptake. These variations should always be considered in the image interpretation.[L1050]

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Fluciclovine (18F) can be increased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: Ranolazine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Fluciclovine (18F) can be increased when it is combined with Ranolazine.

interactions.name: Lumacaftor
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Fluciclovine (18F) can be decreased when it is combined with Lumacaftor.
